content_en:
1.1. How allegiance was sworn to 'Ali (&) o n Friday, 18 Dhul-Wjjah 35 AH,' the Rightly Guided Caliph 'Uthmin ibn 'Af& (&) was martyred at the hands of outlaw rebels, who killed him (&) unlawfully and wrongfully. They had come from various regions and various tribes that were not known for any service to Islam or any good achievement in this world. After this, all of the Companions of the Messenger of Allah (g) who were left in Madinah swore allegiance to 'Ali (&) as caliph. There was no one better than him at that time, so no one else claimed leadership after 'Uthmk (&). 'Ali (&), the father of the two grandsons of the Prophet (&), was not eager for it, and he only accepted it after strong urging from those of the Companions who were left in Madinah, for fear that the tribulations might increase and spread further. Despite that, he was not spared the criticism of some ignorant people as the result of turmoil that took place later on, such as the battles of the Camel and Siffeen, the flames of which were fanned by those who The oath ofallegiance to Xli, his characteristics & his way of life hated Islam, such as Ibn Saba' and his followers, whom he led astray due to their evil nature and the deviation of their hearts from true guidance. The manner in which they selected 'Ali (&) for the caliphate was narrated by some of the scholar^.^ Abu Bakr al-Khalla narrated with his sound chain of narration going back to Muhammad ibn alHanafiyyah that the latter said: "I was with 'Ali (&) when 'Utbmgn (4) was under siege. A man came to 'Ali and said: ' 'Amir alMu'mineen has just been killed.' 'Ah (&) got up, and I grabbed him by the waist, fearing for him. He said: 'Let me go, may you be bereft of your mother!' 'Ali (&) went to the house where 'Uthmsn had been killed, may Allah have mercy on him. He went into his house and shut the door. The people came to him and knocked at the door, then they entered upon him and said: 'This man has been killed, and the people must have a caliph. We do not know of anyone more desenring of that than you.' 'Ali (&) said to them: 'You do not want me; I would rather be an adviser to you than a leader.' They said: 'No, by Allah, we do not know of anyone more deserving of it than you.' He said: 'If you insist on me, then allegiance to me should not be sworn in secret. Rather I will go out to the mosque and accept the people's a~le~iance."'~ According to another report narrated from S a m ibn Abi Ja'd from Muhammad ibn al-Hanafiyyah: "The Companions of the Messenger of AUah came to him and said: 'This man has been killed, and the people must have a leader. We do not find anyone who is more deserving of that than you, or who is more senior in Islam, or closer to the Messenger of AUah (@I.' 'Ali (&) said: 'Do not do it, for I would rather be an adviser to you than a leader.' They said: 'No, by Allah, we will not do anything until we swear allegiance to you.' He said: 'Then (do it) in the mosque, for allegiance to me should not be hidden, and it should only be with the approval of the Muslims.' "

CHAPTER THREE The Oath of Allegiance to 'Ali (&), the most important of his characteristics and his way of life in society
content_id:
Pada hari Jumat, 18 Dzulhijjah tahun 35 H, Khalifah yang lurus 'Utsman ibn 'Affan radhiyallahu 'anhu syahid di tangan para pemberontak yang membunuhnya secara zalim dan tanpa hak. Mereka datang dari berbagai daerah dan kabilah yang tidak dikenal memiliki jasa apa pun dalam Islam. Setelah peristiwa itu, seluruh sahabat Rasulullah shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam yang masih berada di Madinah membai'at Ali radhiyallahu 'anhu sebagai khalifah. Tidak ada yang lebih layak darinya pada saat itu, sehingga tidak ada seorang pun yang mengklaim kepemimpinan setelah 'Utsman radhiyallahu 'anhu.

Ali radhiyallahu 'anhu, ayah dari dua cucu Nabi, tidak berambisi menduduki jabatan itu. Ia hanya menerimanya setelah desakan kuat dari para sahabat yang masih ada di Madinah, karena khawatir fitnah akan semakin bertambah dan menyebar. Meskipun demikian, ia tidak lepas dari kritikan sebagian orang yang tidak mengerti, akibat kekacauan yang terjadi kemudian, seperti Perang Jamal dan Perang Shiffin, yang apinya dinyalakan oleh mereka yang membenci Islam, seperti Ibn Saba' dan para pengikutnya.

Abu Bakr al-Khalla meriwayatkan dengan sanad yang sahih dari Muhammad ibn al-Hanafiyyah bahwa ia berkata: "Aku bersama Ali ketika 'Utsman sedang dalam kepungan. Seorang lelaki datang kepada Ali dan berkata: 'Amirul Mukminin baru saja dibunuh.' Ali berdiri, dan aku memegang pinggangnya karena khawatir untuknya. Ali pergi ke rumah tempat 'Utsman dibunuh, semoga Allah merahmatinya. Ia masuk ke rumahnya dan menutup pintunya. Orang-orang datang dan mengetuk pintu, lalu masuk dan berkata: 'Orang ini telah dibunuh, dan rakyat harus memiliki seorang khalifah. Kami tidak mengetahui siapa yang lebih berhak dari engkau.' Ali berkata kepada mereka: 'Kalian tidak membutuhkan aku; aku lebih suka menjadi penasihat bagimu daripada pemimpin.' Mereka berkata: 'Tidak, demi Allah, kami tidak mengetahui siapa yang lebih berhak dari engkau.' Ali berkata: 'Jika kalian tetap bersikeras, maka baiat kepadaku tidak boleh dilakukan secara rahasia. Aku akan keluar ke masjid dan menerima baiat rakyat.'"

Menurut riwayat lain dari Sa'im ibn Abi Ja'd dari Muhammad ibn al-Hanafiyyah: "Para sahabat Rasulullah datang kepadanya dan berkata: 'Orang ini telah dibunuh, dan rakyat harus memiliki pemimpin. Kami tidak menemukan siapa pun yang lebih berhak dari engkau, atau yang lebih senior dalam Islam, atau lebih dekat kepada Rasulullah.' Ali berkata: 'Jangan lakukan itu, karena aku lebih suka menjadi penasihat bagimu daripada pemimpin.' Mereka berkata: 'Tidak, demi Allah, kami tidak akan melakukan apa pun hingga kami membai'atmu.' Ali berkata: 'Maka lakukanlah di masjid, karena baiat kepadaku tidak boleh disembunyikan, dan harus dilakukan dengan persetujuan kaum muslimin.'" Ini adalah Bab Ketiga tentang Baiat kepada Ali radhiyallahu 'anhu, karakteristik terpenting, dan cara hidupnya dalam masyarakat.
content_ar: 1.1. How allegiance was sworn to 'علي (&) o n Friday, 18 Dhul-Wjjah 35 AH,' the Rightly Guided Caliph 'Uthmin ibn 'Af& (&) was martyred at the hands of outlaw rebels, who killed him (&) unlawfully and wrongfully. They had come from various regions and various tribes that were not known for any service to الإسلام or any الخير achievement in this الدنيا. بعد this, all of the الصحابة of الرسول ﷺ of الله (g) who were left in المدينة المنورة swore allegiance to 'علي (&) as caliph. There was no one better than him at that time, لذا no one else claimed leadership بعد 'Uthmk (&). 'علي (&), the الأب of the two grandsons of النبي ﷺ (&), was not eager for it, and he only accepted it بعد strong urging from those of the الصحابة who were left in المدينة المنورة, for الخوف that the tribulations might increase and spread further. رغم that, he was not spared the criticism of some ignorant people as the result of turmoil that took place later on, مثل the battles of the Camel and Siffeen, the flames of which were fanned by those who The oath ofallegiance to Xli, his characteristics & his الطريق of الحياة hated الإسلام, مثل Ibn Saba' and his followers, whom he led astray بسبب their الشر nature and the deviation of their القلوب from true الهداية. The manner in which they selected 'علي (&) for the الخلافة was narrated by some of the scholar^.^ أبو بكر al-Khalla narrated with his sound chain of narration going back to محمد ﷺ ibn alHanafiyyah that the latter said: "I was with 'علي (&) عندما 'Utbmgn (4) was under siege. A man came to 'علي and said: ' 'Amir alMu'mineen has just been killed.' 'Ah (&) got up, and I grabbed him by the waist, fearing for him. قال: 'Let me go, may you be bereft of your الأم!' 'علي (&) went to the house where 'Uthmsn had been killed, may الله have الرحمة on him. He went into his house and shut the door. The people came to him and knocked at the door, then they entered upon him and said: 'This man has been killed, and the people must have a caliph. We do not know of anyone more desenring of that than you.' 'علي (&) said to them: 'You do not want me; I would rather be an adviser to you than a leader.' قالوا: 'No, by الله, we do not know of anyone more deserving of it than you.' قال: 'إذا you insist on me, then allegiance to me should not be sworn in secret. Rather I will go out to the المسجد and accept the people's a~le~iance."'~ وفقاً لـ another report narrated from S a m ibn Abi Ja'd from محمد ﷺ ibn al-Hanafiyyah: "The الصحابة of الرسول ﷺ of AUah came to him and said: 'This man has been killed, and the people must have a leader. We do not find anyone who is more deserving of that than you, or who is more senior in الإسلام, or closer to الرسول ﷺ of AUah (@I.' 'علي (&) said: 'Do not do it, for I would rather be an adviser to you than a leader.' قالوا: 'No, by الله, we will not do anything حتى we swear allegiance to you.' قال: 'Then (do it) in the المسجد, for allegiance to me should not be
